title: dodonaea viscosa f. minor
intro: Dodonaea viscosa f. minor, also known as minor hopbush, is a variant of the Dodonaea viscosa plant, a shrub native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The leaves of Dodonaea viscosa f. minor are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
